skill.name: skill-writer
skill.description: Guide authors through creating focused Claude Code Agent Skills with valid SKILL.md frontmatter, clear instructions, examples, validation checks, and troubleshooting steps.
skill.summary: Create and validate Claude Code Agent Skills with clear structure and practical authoring guidance.
skill.author: Dami-Randle

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but the reviewed file is documentation for writing Claude Code skills. The command and filesystem hits are examples for creating or validating skills, and the weak-crypto hits are false positives from ordinary prose such as description text. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE as documentation, LOW risk in context. The skill includes bash examples for creating directories, installing placeholder packages, listing files, checking YAML, running Claude debug mode, and invoking example helper scripts. These are authoring instructions, not hidden executable code, but agents using the skill may run local commands when a user asks to create or test a skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly present in markdown code blocks. The surrounding text clearly frames them as skill-authoring examples, so the security impact is limited to user-approved local authoring workflows.

low_findings.title: Personal And Project Skill Path Examples
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE as documentation, LOW risk in context. The skill references ~/.claude/skills and .claude/skills as valid skill locations. This is expected for a Claude Code skill-authoring guide and does not access hidden files by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-path references are visible examples of official skill locations. No code reads, writes, or exfiltrates files without user intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The static weak-cryptography detections occur in normal documentation text and YAML examples, not in cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, password storage, or security-sensitive crypto operation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain prose or sample descriptions, not cryptographic functions or dependencies. I found no evidence of weak crypto implementation in the only reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Scanner Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The lines flagged for reconnaissance are checklist and validation text about common pitfalls, YAML validity, and description requirements. They do not collect system information or enumerate sensitive host state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation checklist items. There is no command or instruction that gathers host, user, network, credential, or process details.

content.value_statement: Writing Claude Code skills requires clear scope, valid frontmatter, and testable instructions. This skill guides authors through structure, examples, validation, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through defining a focused skill scope and trigger conditions.
Explains where personal and project skills should be stored.
Provides the required SKILL.md frontmatter fields and naming rules.
Shows recommended Markdown sections for instructions, examples, and requirements.
Includes validation and troubleshooting checklists for skill activation problems.
Describes common patterns for read-only, script-based, and multi-file skills.
limitations: It is a documentation guide, not an automated skill generator by itself.
It targets Claude Code Agent Skills and may not match every marketplace format.
It does not validate files with a parser or schema tool on its own.
Script examples are placeholders and require user-provided implementation details.

use_cases.title: Create a personal workflow skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated personal workflow into a focused skill with clear activation language and a valid SKILL.md file.

use_cases.title: Standardize team skill authoring
use_cases.description: Give contributors a shared checklist for naming, frontmatter, examples, and testing before committing project skills.

use_cases.title: Debug skill discovery issues
use_cases.description: Review skill paths, descriptions, YAML syntax, and activation phrases when Claude Code does not use a skill.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a basic skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Claude Code skill for [workflow]. Ask the key scope questions, then suggest a name, description, and SKILL.md outline.

prompt_templates.title: Write the SKILL.md file
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a defined skill idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete SKILL.md for a skill that [does task]. Include valid frontmatter, clear instructions, examples, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md draft for clarity, activation quality, frontmatter validity, and missing edge cases. Suggest concrete edits.

prompt_templates.title: Design a multi-file skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced author building references or scripts
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me structure a multi-file Claude Code skill for [domain]. Decide what belongs in SKILL.md, references, examples, scripts, and templates.

output_examples.input: I want a skill for reviewing database migrations.
output_examples.output: A focused skill plan with a hyphenated name, a discovery-ready description, required SKILL.md sections, validation checks, and suggested examples.

output_examples.input: My skill does not activate when I ask Claude Code for help.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist that reviews location, YAML frontmatter, trigger words, specific file types, and restart or debug steps.

output_examples.input: Convert this recurring prompt into a reusable team skill.
output_examples.output: A scoped skill structure that separates quick instructions, detailed references, examples, dependencies, and testing guidance.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one capability with specific trigger words.
Use valid YAML frontmatter and ensure the skill name matches its directory.
Test activation with realistic user prompts before sharing the skill.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that cover many unrelated workflows.
Do not use vague descriptions such as general document or data help.
Do not include script dependencies without documenting how users should install and test them.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code Agent Skills with valid frontmatter, clear instructions, examples, and validation steps.

faq.question: Can it create both personal and project skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use personal skill folders and when to use project skill folders.

faq.question: Does it run validation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides validation checklists and command examples, but it does not include an automated validator.

faq.question: Can it help with multi-file skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes optional reference, example, script, and template files for progressive disclosure.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude Code?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for Claude Code Agent Skills, though some authoring ideas apply to Codex workflows.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the workflow goal, expected trigger phrases, needed tools, dependencies, and whether the skill is personal or shared.
